mechanism_narrative: With the federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.07%), the discount rate applied to equity cash flows remains structurally compressed, sustaining a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 valuations over rolling 20-day windows regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample mean (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: As UNRATE approaches the historically referenced 6.5% Fed forward-guidance threshold, markets begin pricing accelerated policy normalization, producing a short-horizon negative reaction in SPX as investors front-run anticipated rate liftoff risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls to or below 6.5% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=6.6 as of t-1mo, near 6.5% Evans rule threshold
regime_hint: late zero-lower-bound period with forward guidance thresholds
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following UNRATE crossing 6.5% are positive in more than 55% of historical crossings, or Rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signals broad-based consumer demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; when both accelerate together, forward 20-day SPX returns are higher than when only one rises, reflecting confirmed rather than isolated demand momentum.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month change > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=427829 and HOUST=999 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration signal underperforms the single-series RSAFS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC over any 3-year window, or produces no statistically significant return differential (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding aggregate supply capacity and corporate output, which mechanically feeds into higher forward earnings expectations and positive SPX drift over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing 3-month INDPRO growth and forward 60d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or INDPRO uptrends coincide with negative SPX returns in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound regime undergoing QE tapering with subdued inflation (CPIAUCSL growth low), continued balance-sheet reduction combined with rate expectations anchored at zero creates asymmetric downside risk for equities over a quarterly horizon, as liquidity withdrawal outweighs the low-rate tailwind once taper signals intensify.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.07, CPIAUCSL=234.582 as of snapshot, Fed tapering QE since Dec 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound with active QE tapering
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during identified taper regimes are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the regime-conditional signal does not outperform an unconditional FEDFUNDS baseline in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth accelerates concurrently with a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE), the joint signal confirms genuine labor market tightening rather than statistical noise from labor force participation shifts, driving positive forward 20-day SPX returns via improved consumption and earnings outlooks.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change > 150k AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=137499 thousand, UNRATE=6.6 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: labor market tightening phase of expansion
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS+UNRATE signal fails to produce Rank IC above 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or single-series PAYEMS-only model matches or exceeds joint-signal performance.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below its recent trailing trend signals weakening aggregate demand momentum that markets have not yet fully priced, leading to negative SPX drift over the following quarter as growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2%
failure_condition: SPX forward 60d returns following sub-2% GDPC1 growth prints are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or GDP revisions systematically overturn the deceleration signal within the same quarter.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession expansion regime characterized by accelerating industrial production and low, stable inflation (CPIAUCSL growth subdued), rising output does not trigger margin-compressing input cost pressure, allowing supply-side growth to translate more fully into positive forward equity returns over a quarterly horizon than it would in a high-inflation regime.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=101.0251, CPIAUCSL=234.582 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: post-recession expansion with low and stable inflation
failure_condition: The INDPRO-return relationship in low-inflation regimes shows no significant improvement in Rank IC versus high-inflation regimes across historical comparison windows, or returns are negative despite INDPRO acceleration in more than 45% of low-inflation instances.
